item.title: SIX U.S. SOLDIERS WHO SURVIVED IRANIAN DRONE STRIKE IN KUWAIT ACCUSE ARMY LEADERS OF IGNORING REPEATED WARNINGS THAT BASE WAS VULNERABLE; ALLEGE COMMANDERS POSITIONED TROOPS IN UNSAFE LOCATION WITHOUT
item.description: pSIX U.S. SOLDIERS WHO SURVIVED IRANIAN DRONE STRIKE IN KUWAIT ACCUSE ARMY LEADERS OF IGNORING REPEATED WARNINGS THAT BASE WAS VULNERABLE; ALLEGE COMMANDERS POSITIONED TROOPS IN UNSAFE LOCATION WITHOUT ADEQUATE DRONE DEFENCES AND ABANDONED BUILDING AFTER STRIKE - WAPO .../p

item.title: Thailand’s Central Bank and SEC Probe High-Value USDT Transactions According to The Nation, the Bank of Thailand will require individuals depositing THB 5 million ($150,000) or more in cash to verify
item.description: pThailand’s Central Bank and SEC Probe High-Value USDT Transactionsbr /br /According to The Nation, the Bank of Thailand will require individuals depositing THB 5 million ($150,000) or more in cash to verify the source of funds. It is also working with Thailand’s SEC to review large stablecoin trans

item.title: A government that borrows in its own currency rarely has to default. It can always print instead. Total global debt has climbed above 300% of GDP, and the cost of carrying it rises as yields climb. Cu
item.description: pA government that borrows in its own currency rarely has to default. It can always print instead. br /br /Total global debt has climbed above 300% of GDP, and the cost of carrying it rises as yields climb. Cutting spending is politically punishing and printing has become the way out. br /br /Mone
